ads.headline: Lead generation interest surging; $25 budgets need laser-focused targeting now
actions: Audit your audience targeting to exclude low-intent users bleeding your $25 daily budget
Test lead magnet ads (free quotes, checklists) instead of direct service ads this week
Create separate $10 awareness + $15 conversion campaigns to maximize lead quality at small budget
ads.source: Google Trends lead generation spike (15 interest) + Meta Ads subreddit small budget discussions

creative.headline: Instagram Reels dominance is real—video content strategy beats static posts
actions: Film a 15-second Meta ads fail/win comparison Reel showing before/after client results
Create a Reel series: 'One Meta ads mistake service businesses make daily'
Use trending audio + text overlay on Reels—hook viewer in first 3 seconds with a question or pattern interrupt
creative.source: Google Trends: Instagram Reels (30) vs video hook (0) + content strategy (16)—shows audience searching for Reels but not hook techniques, indicating a content gap

critic.headline: Most failing ads this week have no clear offer — just a service name
actions: Open your current ad — does it have a specific outcome and deadline, or just your service name?
If your CTA is 'Learn More' or 'Contact Us' — change it to 'Get a Free Quote' or similar action
Check your click-to-lead rate: if under 15%, your offer is the problem, not the audience

competitors.headline: Service businesses running before/after photos are dominating the library
actions: Open Meta Ad Library → search your niche → filter active ads → look for photos not graphics
The most common pattern: real job photo + specific result + deadline offer
Film or photograph your next client's job — that's your next winning creative
